params.text:
Call 999 and ask for a defibrillator

If the person is unresponsive and not breathing normally, call 999 without delay. Put your phone on speaker so your hands are free, and the call handler can guide you through CPR.

Tell the operator clearly where you are and that someone has collapsed and is not breathing normally. Ask whether there is a public access defibrillator (AED) nearby, and if someone else is present, send them to fetch it while you stay with the person.

Do not leave the person to look for an AED yourself if you are alone. Starting and continuing chest compressions is more important than searching for equipment. If you are on your own, keep going with CPR and let the 999 handler direct you.

params.taskDescription: Drag the words into the gaps to complete the guidance on calling for help.
params.textField: If the person is unresponsive and not breathing normally, call *999* without delay. Put your phone on *speaker* so your hands stay free. Ask whether there is a nearby *defibrillator* and send *someone* else to fetch it. If you are *alone*, do not leave the person — keep doing chest *compressions*.
